content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 19, 1994

Peter A. Bagatelos
Bagatelos & Fadem
601 California Street, Ste. 1801
San Francisco, CA 94108

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-94-275

Dear Mr. Bagatelos:

You have requested confirmation of telephone assistance
provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Because your questions are
general in nature, we will treat your letter as a request for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

Your letter, dated August 16, 1994, accurately summarizes my
advice concerning disclosure of subvendor payments pursuant to
Section 84303 and Regulation 18431. With regard to payments made
by a direct mail company to a separate mailing house, absent
specific facts, such payments must be itemized as subvendor
payments. In addition, as provided in Regulation 18431(a)(2)(F),
payments for postage for campaign mailings must be disclosed.

If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: With regard to payments made by a direct mail company to a separate mailing house, absent specific facts, such payments must be itemized as subvendor payments. In addition, as provided in Regulation 18431(a)(2)(F), payments for postage for campaign mailings must be disclosed.
sections.question_synthetic: How should a direct mail company disclose subvendor payments made to a separate mailing house, and what disclosure requirements apply to postage costs for campaign mailings?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Payments from a direct mail company to a separate mailing house must be itemized as subvendor payments under Section 84303 and Regulation 18431, and postage costs for campaign mailings must also be disclosed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter confirming prior telephone guidance; no formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present, but clear advice provided in body of letter regarding Political Reform Act disclosure obligations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should a direct mail company disclose subvendor payments made to a separate mailing house, and what disclosure requirements apply to postage costs for campaign mailings?

CONCLUSION: With regard to payments made by a direct mail company to a separate mailing house, absent specific facts, such payments must be itemized as subvendor payments. In addition, as provided in Regulation 18431(a)(2)(F), payments for postage for campaign mailings must be disclosed.

With regard to payments made by a direct mail company to a separate mailing house, absent specific facts, such payments must be itemized as subvendor payments. In addition, as provided in Regulation 18431(a)(2)(F), payments for postage for campaign mailings must be disclosed. If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5662. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding campaign disclosure requirements for subvendor payments and postage costs in connection with direct mail campaign activities. The letter clarifies that payments to mailing houses must be itemized as subvendor payments and postage must be disclosed.
